_comment: Generic reference example (fictional). Shows the token structure + tone. SKIP_DEMO_SHOT leaves the page-2 frame blank; set DEMO_URL and remove SKIP_DEMO_SHOT for a real build.
COVER_SUB: A 90-day plan to turn the funnel we built into a steady, predictable flow of booked strategy calls.
DIAGNOSIS_HEADLINE: Almost everything is already in place.
DIAGNOSIS_BODY: Northbeam has spent years building a service that clients stay with and refer. Delivery is strong and the close rate on live calls is high. What slowed sits at the top of the funnel: the referrals and word of mouth that used to fill it have quietened, and no structured system has stepped in to replace that flow. That is the one piece left to build, and it is the piece we do.
STAT1_LABEL: In business, with a proven, referable service.
STAT2_LABEL: Close rate once a conversation starts.
STAT3_LABEL: New clients today, against room for far more.
DEMO_INTRO: Before we ever spoke, we rebuilt the Northbeam offer as a live, branded funnel, the same structure that acquires our own clients. Here it is:
GAP1_WHAT: A focused hour together, combining your years of knowing this client with our pattern library from running dozens of funnels, to engineer the service into a cold-traffic-ready offer people actually opt in for.
GAP1_WHY: The best-designed page with a weak offer gets no opt-ins. The offer is the single biggest lever on the whole funnel, and it is the hardest thing to see from inside the business.
GAP1_RESULT: A message cold prospects respond to. The difference between wasted spend and a calendar that fills.
GAP2_WHAT: We script and edit the video sales letter for you, on a proven structure: hook, problem, mechanism, proof, call to action. No writing or filming burden on your side.
GAP2_WHY: You close well once you are on the call, but that warming only happens after a booking, and it does not scale. The VSL does the warming before the call, at scale, so prospects arrive already sold.
GAP2_RESULT: Higher show-up, higher close, less selling to do live, and the same close rate holding up on strangers, not only referrals.
GAP3_WHAT: We build and run your Meta ads end to end, the scalable engine that carries your guaranteed calls. You fund the spend (from $50 a day), we handle the creative, targeting, testing and daily optimisation.
GAP3_BONUS: On top, free, we reactivate the audience you already own, your CRM, LinkedIn and email, with a newsletter that lowers your cost per lead.
GAP3_RESULT: The booked calls we guarantee, from an engine you can scale up whenever you want more.
ROI_INTRO: This deliberately assumes a close rate of 40 percent, well under the real 55 to 60, and a conservative average client value. Only the five booked calls are guaranteed.
ROI_KICKER: And that is the floor. At the close rate actually achieved, five calls is closer to 3 clients, around $18,000, roughly 6 times over. That is just the guaranteed minimum, the ad engine scales past it, and every multi-year client compounds well beyond this table.
PRICE_EXPLANATION: We bill in GBP. The £2,500 is roughly $1,000 a month to build and run the whole system and conservatively generate around $12,000 from the guaranteed calls. Your ad spend is separate and yours, from $50 a day, and it is the fuel that produces the leads.
FAQ1_Q: "Why would paid traffic work when referrals have slowed?"
FAQ1_A: Because it is not just spend pointed at a page. We fix the offer, warm every visitor with a VSL, and manage the ads day to day, so the traffic converts instead of leaking. Referrals were never an engine you could scale; paid traffic, run properly, is.
FAQ2_Q: "Do I have to pay for ads on top of the fee?"
FAQ2_A: Yes, and that is the point. The ad spend is your budget and your leads, from $50 a day up, and it is what makes the calls scalable and the guarantee real. The fee covers building and running the whole system, and we reactivate your existing audience free on top to keep your cost per lead down.
FAQ3_Q: "How would I actually use the funnel day to day?"
FAQ3_A: You do not manage it. We build it, drive traffic to it, and qualified prospects land on your calendar. Your only job stays the part you are already excellent at: the call.
FAQ4_Q: "Can I just take the free page and run with it?"
FAQ4_A: The page is yours either way. On its own it is a shell, with no offer, no video and no traffic. The engagement is what turns it into something that books calls.
FAQ5_Q: "I want to look over the funnel before I commit."
FAQ5_A: Completely fair, and exactly what this document and Thursday's call are for. Take your time with it, then give a straight answer.
NEXT_STEP_BODY: We speak again Thursday. Take a proper look at the funnel and this proposal beforehand. On the call we will answer anything still open, and the one thing we ask is a clear yes or no, so we both plan our time well. If it is a yes, the seven-day build starts that day. If it is a no, no hard feelings, and the page is yours to keep.
